summary: Phase 4 (Syllabus Tracker + Regulatory Radar + Content Hub + Weekly Recap) verified. Backend: 22/22 pytest cases PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_phase4_content.py covering syllabus list/detail/progress upsert, radar alerts filter+summary+dismiss, content posts+digest, weekly recap, openapi paths, account export includes syllabus_progress + dismissed_alerts, security headers regression, fresh-user journey_level=null path. Frontend: /dashboard now shows all 4 new bento cards (syllabus/radar/hub/recap) with real data; sidebar shows 3 new links plus 5 existing; /syllabus renders 'Where you stand.' heading, 6 Intermediate papers, all 5 filter chips, paper accordion expand+chapter status control click PERSISTS to backend (verified 4→5 mastered after click on I1 CH14); /radar renders 18 alerts (auth-filtered by Intermediate level) with dismiss buttons; /hub renders 20 posts + 13 tag filters + editorial magazine layout; /hub/:slug renders body markdown + 3 related posts + TOC after Helmet bug fix.

ui_bugs.issue: Helmet <title> received JSX children instead of single string, causing ErrorBoundary to catch and REPLACE the entire hub post page with fallback UI. Original: <title>{post.title} · The CA Grid</title>. Fixed by wrapping in template literal: <title>{`${post.title} · The CA Grid`}</title>. Also defensively coalesced post.excerpt to empty string for meta description/og.

integration_issues.issue: A newly signed-up user with journey_level=null is REDIRECTED by a global route guard to /onboarding when navigating to /syllabus, so the [data-testid='syllabus-empty-onboarding'] block is never rendered even though its code path exists in Syllabus.jsx. The review request explicitly wants that empty state to be visible on /syllabus. Current UX (redirect straight to onboarding) is arguably better, but the testid target is unreachable from user flow. Main agent to decide: (a) remove the auto-redirect for /syllabus, or (b) drop the empty-state requirement from spec.

action_items: Decide whether /syllabus should render syllabus-empty-onboarding empty state for journey_level=null users, or whether the current auto-redirect to /onboarding satisfies the requirement (remove the empty-state block from Syllabus.jsx if the latter).
Consider adding a `data-testid='syllabus-heading'` on the h1 for cleaner heading assertions (currently the heading is split across spans which makes Playwright text_content unreliable).

Consider making POST /api/seed also RESET demo user's syllabus progress back to seeded I1=4-mastered / I2=6-in-progress (currently seed only force-reseeds papers if empty; user progress is preserved — noted in review context but worth restating for future regression stability).
critical_code_review_comments: HubPost.jsx Helmet <title> bug: React-Helmet requires a single string child inside <title>. Any interpolation like `<title>{a} literal</title>` produces an array of children and throws Invariant Violation, which is then caught by the top-level ErrorBoundary and replaces the entire route with a fallback. Fix applied — but any future page using Helmet must do the same template-literal pattern (or move to native document.title effect).

server.py is now 2755 lines (was 2244 in phase 3.5). Phase 4 added ~500 LoC of syllabus/radar/content/recap routes inline. This is well past the 700-line guideline; strongly recommend splitting into /app/backend/routers/syllabus.py, radar.py, content.py, recap.py in Phase 5.

Radar summary is cached per-user in memory 60s and content digest 300s. Cache lives in-process; if you scale to multiple workers, users could see divergent counts. Acceptable for MVP but flag for Phase-5 hardening (redis or short-TTL mongo materialised view).
POST /api/syllabus/progress upsert semantics verified — same (user_id, paper_code, chapter_id) tuple returns exactly one row across status transitions. Good.

GET /api/syllabus/progress for a user with journey_level=null returns all 16 papers with zeroed aggregates rather than an empty list. This is a backend design choice; the frontend then relies on its own guard to redirect. Consider tightening the backend to return [] for null-level users so anyone hitting the API directly gets a signal.
Account export shape now includes syllabus_progress[] and dismissed_alerts[] arrays — GDPR-lite regression intact. Verified via TestRegression::test_account_export_includes_new_collections.

Radar dismissed alerts are excluded from GET /api/radar/alerts (auth) but NOT from GET /api/radar/summary immediately due to 60s cache; documented in review notes — verified via GET /api/radar/alerts uncached path after dismiss.

success_rate.frontend: 95% — all Phase 4 pages render + interact after HubPost Helmet fix; only unmet spec is fresh-user empty state which is preempted by a route guard

test_credentials: demo@cagrid.in / demo123 (verified). No new credentials required for Phase 4.
seed_data_creation: Two throwaway signup users created during pytest (TestSyllabusEmptyStateFreshUser + Playwright fresh-user test) — both deleted via /api/account/delete at end of their tests. Demo user's I1 syllabus progress was temporarily mutated during Playwright interaction test (added 1 mastered chapter ch_18016c6b82) and RESET back to not_started at end. I3 upsert-no-duplicate test cleaned up after itself. rad_budget26_ltcg was dismissed for demo user during radar test; it will re-appear only if the seed is re-run (POST /api/seed also reseeds dismissed_alerts to []).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 backend is fully stable; test file /app/backend/tests/test_phase4_content.py runs in ~2s serial (-n 0). The Helmet bug in HubPost.jsx was fixed inside this testing iteration (only ONE Helmet usage in the whole app — HubPost.jsx line ~116). No other pages use Helmet. Rate limits still active — for the fresh-user empty-state test we bypassed rate-limited signup by using page.request.post which runs from a fresh client IP; if you hit 429, wait 60s or reuse demo user. Radar test dismisses 'rad_budget26_ltcg' — if you need it back for a subsequent test, hit POST /api/seed OR mongosh: db.user_dismissed_alerts.deleteMany({user_id:'user_4cb8c3c6f572'}).
rca of the issue: HubPost.jsx Helmet Invariant Violation RCA — react-helmet-async's <title> component uses React.Children.forEach and requires the children resolve to exactly one string. When JSX renders `<title>{post.title} · The CA Grid</title>`, React creates two children (an interpolated string + a literal string), so Helmet's mapChildrenToProps throws 'Helmet expects a string as a child of <title>'. The exception is caught by <ErrorBoundary> in App.js and the whole /hub/:slug route renders the fallback UI instead of the post page. FIX: wrap the entire <title> content in a template literal so only ONE string child is produced: `<title>{`${post.title} · The CA Grid`}</title>`. Verified post-fix — page loads, title, body markdown, 3 related posts, and 6 TOC entries all rendered correctly.
